engineCodes.h2: Land Rover Engine Codes & Average Rebuilt Prices (UK Supply)
engineCodes.h3: Compare costs for every major Land Rover engine – TDV6, Ingenium, V8, Td5, and legacy units. All prices are typical UK market ranges for professionally rebuilt engines with warranty. Click "Get Quote" on any engine to receive tailored prices from our network of UK Land Rover specialists.

groups.name: Modern Ingenium Diesel
groups.failureNote: Common failure points (appears in expandable note): AJ200D/204DTD Ingenium engines are known for timing chain stretch, DPF clogging, and turbocharger failure, often before 80,000 miles. Our rebuilt units include upgraded timing chain kits and revised oil pump assemblies. The AJ20D6 3.0L Ingenium suffers crankshaft bearing wear in early models; rebuilds include uprated bearing shells and modified oil supply lines.

engines.compatibleModels: Discovery Sport (L550), Range Rover Evoque (L538), Discovery 5, Velar, Range Rover Sport
Discovery Sport, Range Rover Evoque (2015–2019), Jaguar XE/XF
Range Rover Evoque, Discovery Sport, Velar
Range Rover (L405), Range Rover Sport (L494), Discovery 5

groups.name: TDV6 Diesel
groups.failureNote: Common failure points: The 276DT 2.7 TDV6 is notorious for crankshaft failure, oil pump housing disintegration, and snapped timing belts - often causing catastrophic engine seizure. The 306DT/306DTX 3.0 TDV6 suffers similar crankshaft bearing issues plus EGR valve and turbo actuator failures. All our rebuilt TDV6 engines include uprated crankshaft bearings, revised oil pump assemblies, and upgraded EGR components.

engines.compatibleModels: Discovery 4 (L319), Range Rover Sport (L320, 2009–2013), Range Rover (L322)
Discovery 4, Range Rover Sport (2012–2018), Range Rover (L405)

groups.name: V8 Petrol
groups.failureNote: Common failure points: The 508PN 5.0L Supercharged V8 is prone to timing chain tensioner failure (rattling on cold start), water pump leakage, and supercharger coupler wear. The 448PN 4.4L V8 suffers timing chain guide rail disintegration and VANOS solenoid failure. Our rebuilt V8 engines include reinforced timing chain kits, updated tensioners, and new water pumps as standard.

engines.compatibleModels: Range Rover (L322/L405), Range Rover Sport (L320/L494), Discovery 4

groups.name: Defender Diesel
groups.failureNote: Common failure points: The 11L 200Tdi and 23L 300Tdi are generally robust but suffer head gasket failure, timing belt wear, and oil leaks from ageing seals. The ZSD-424/244DT Puma (Ford DuraTorq) engines have known injector seal failures, EGR valve clogging, and VNT turbo mechanism sticking. Our rebuilt Defender engines include upgraded head gaskets, new injector seals, and refreshed turbo assemblies where specified.

engines.compatibleModels: Defender Station Wagon, Pickup, Cabrio, Platform/Chassis

groups.name: Additional & Specialist Engines
groups.failureNote: Common failure points: The 224DT 2.2L diesel is generally reliable but suffers EGR valve issues, turbo oil feed pipe blockages, and intermittent DPF regeneration problems. The M52B28 (BMW-sourced) can experience VANOS seal degradation, cooling system weakness, and oil pump nut loosening on high-mileage examples. commonProblems.h2: Land Rover Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: From timing chain failures in the Ingenium diesels to catastrophic crankshaft seizures in the TDV6, Land Rover engines carry well-documented reliability risks across their range. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point — plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below are the six most common issues our UK network sees, with realistic repair options and price bands for each. Compare quotes to find the right balance of cost, reliability and turnaround for your Land Rover.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Failure – Why Land Rover Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Discovery Sport (L550), Range Rover Evoque (L538), Discovery 5, Velar, Range Rover Sport (2015–present, AJ200D, 204DTD, 204DTA)
problems.rootCause: The 2.0 Ingenium diesel uses two timing chains lubricated by engine oil. Oil dilution from incomplete DPF regenerations degrades lubricant quality, causing the chain to stretch and plastic tensioner rails to wear. Once the chain jumps teeth or snaps, pistons strike valves — catastrophic engine failure follows.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Both timing chains, guides, tensioners, idlers, seals, oil and filter change. Engine‑in‑situ where possible.
repairOptions.longevity: 2–5 years. Only viable before the chain snaps or severe rattle develops.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full chain system replacement with engine removed. Includes valve inspection and minor head work if light piston contact has occurred.
repairOptions.longevity: 3–7 years. Appropriate where damage is moderate but bottom end is still sound.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Professionally rebuilt Ingenium unit with upgraded timing chain kit, revised tensioners, new oil pump, bench testing. Supply & fit included.
repairOptions.longevity: 5–10+ years. Eliminates the weak point and resets engine life. Far cheaper than a main‑dealer new unit.

problems.recommendation: If your Ingenium is still rattling only on cold start and within the preventative window, a specialist chain replacement at £1,200–£2,500 is the sensible route. However, once the chain has snapped or severe oil dilution has occurred, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components almost always delivers better long‑term value than an expensive engine‑out repair of a damaged unit. On a Discovery Sport worth £8,000–£14,000, a £2,500–£5,000 rebuilt engine makes clear financial sense compared to a £8,000+ dealer replacement.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt Ingenium engine prices for your Land Rover

problems.h4: Crankshaft & Bearing Failure – The TDV6 Engine's Critical Weakness
problems.affectedModels: Discovery 3 (L319), Discovery 4 (L319), Range Rover Sport (L320, 2005–2013), Range Rover (L322/L405, 2013–2018) (276DT 2.7, 306DT 3.0, 306DTX SDV6)
problems.rootCause: The TDV6 crankshaft is compact and narrow by design, creating weak points between the bearing journals and counterweights. When main bearings spin or seize — typically from oil starvation, extended service intervals, or blocked oil pickup strainers — the crankshaft snaps, destroying the engine beyond repair. Early warning signs are rare; many engines fail suddenly during normal driving.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement – Reconditioned Long Engine (2.7 TDV6)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Fully reconditioned long engine with new or reground crankshaft, pistons, bearings, oil pump, timing belts, refurbished heads. Supply & fit included. 12‑month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: 5–10+ years. The only viable fix for a snapped crankshaft.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement – Reconditioned Long Engine (3.0 TDV6/SDV6)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Same as above, with upgraded bearing shells and modified oil supply lines addressing the original design weakness. Hot‑tested before installation.
repairOptions.longevity: 5–10+ years. Includes upgrades to prevent repeat failure.

problems.recommendation: There is no minor fix for a TDV6 crankshaft failure — once the bearing spins or the crank snaps, the only reliable solution is a fully reconditioned long engine. The specialist route saves 50–60% versus main dealer pricing while delivering a unit that addresses the root design weakness. For a Discovery 4 worth £10,000–£16,000, a £6,500–£7,700 rebuilt engine is unquestionably the right financial decision versus writing the vehicle off.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for rebuilt TDV6 / SDV6 Land Rover engines with uprated bearings

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure – Why the AJ133 V8 Needs Early Intervention
problems.affectedModels: Range Rover (L322/L405), Range Rover Sport (L320/L494), Discovery 4, SVAutobiography (2009–present, 508PN / 508PS)
problems.rootCause: The AJ133 5.0-litre supercharged V8 uses plastic timing chain tensioners that degrade with heat cycles and oil contamination. A distinctive cold‑start rattle is the first warning. Left unaddressed, chain slack causes skipped timing — pistons strike valves, destroying the engine. Supercharger coupler wear and water pump leakage frequently accompany tensioner failure at higher mileages.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing chain kit (3 chains, tensioners, guides), new water pump, supercharger coupler inspection, oil service. Engine‑out procedure.
repairOptions.longevity: 5–8 years. Essential on any AJ133 approaching 60k miles with cold‑start rattle.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement – Rebuilt / Reconditioned V8 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete remanufactured unit with upgraded tensioners, reinforced timing components, new water pump, tested supercharger assembly. Supply & fit.
repairOptions.longevity: 5–10+ years. The only safe route if the chain has already skipped or secondary damage is found.

problems.recommendation: If you hear the cold‑start rattle, act immediately — a £2,500–£3,500 specialist chain replacement preserves your engine for another 5–8 years and costs a fraction of what follows if ignored. However, once the chain has jumped timing or the engine has been run low on oil, a rebuilt unit with upgraded components is the surest path back to reliability. On a Range Rover Sport worth £15,000–£25,000, a rebuilt engine at £5,500–£8,500 is far more sensible than a £25,000+ dealer replacement or writing the car off.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt 5.0 Supercharged V8 engine prices for your Range Rover

problems.h4: Turbocharger Failure – A Common Problem Across Land Rover Diesel Engines
problems.affectedModels: Discovery Sport, Evoque, Velar (Ingenium 2.0), Freelander 2, Evoque 2.2 (224DT), Defender Puma (ZSD‑424 / 244DT), Discovery 4, Range Rover Sport (SDV6 3.0)
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil‑feed pipes carbonise over time, restricting lubrication to the turbo bearings. On Ingenium engines, contaminated oil from DPF dilution accelerates the wear. On Defender Puma engines, the variable geometry turbo (VNT) mechanism sticks from carbon buildup. On SDV6 engines, turbo actuators fail and oil seals leak. A disintegrating turbo can send metal fragments through the entire oil system — turning a £1,000 turbo job into a £5,000+ engine replacement.

repairOptions.tier: Minor – Recon turbo with new oil‑feed pipe
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Reconditioned or exchange turbocharger, new oil‑supply line, oil and filter change.
repairOptions.longevity: 2–5 years. Works if failure was purely turbo‑side with no debris spread into the engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Both turbochargers reconditioned, new actuators, oil‑feed pipes, intercooler flush, full service.
repairOptions.longevity: 3–7 years. Recommended when one turbo has already failed; the second is likely close behind.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement – Rebuilt Engine with Guaranteed Turbo Integrity
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuild including inspected/replaced turbocharger, new oil‑feed pipes, cleaned intercooler and intake tract. Supply & fit.
repairOptions.longevity: 5–10+ years. Essential if metal fragments from the turbo have circulated through the engine oil.

problems.recommendation: If you heard a siren‑like whine followed by loss of power and heavy smoke, assume oil contamination. In that scenario, replacing only the turbo is a short‑term gamble — a rebuilt engine with guaranteed turbo integrity is safer and often no more expensive than a turbo swap that later reveals bearing damage. For older vehicles like the Freelander 2 or Defender Puma with isolated turbo failure, a specialist recon turbo at £550–£1,200 is the pragmatic choice.
problems.cta: → Find rebuilt Land Rover diesel engines with guaranteed turbo integrity

problems.h4: DPF Regeneration & Oil Dilution – The Hidden Ingenium Engine Killer
problems.affectedModels: Discovery Sport, Range Rover Evoque, Discovery 5, Velar, Range Rover Sport (2015–present, AJ200D / 204DTD / 204DTA)
problems.rootCause: The DPF is positioned too far from the engine to achieve sufficient temperature for passive regeneration. This forces frequent active regenerations, during which unburnt diesel washes past the piston rings into the sump. The resulting oil dilution degrades lubricant quality, accelerating timing chain wear, camshaft bearing damage, and ultimately complete engine failure. This disproportionately affects UK owners who use their vehicles for shorter journeys or urban driving.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: (DPF) £250 – £500 (EGR) / £300 – £800 (DPF clean) EGR valve replacement or DPF forced regeneration / chemical cleaning.
repairOptions.longevity: 1–3 years. Treats symptoms. Recurrence likely on town‑driven cars.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate – Combined EGR + DPF + chain tensioner
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New EGR cooler, DPF chemical clean or replacement, timing chain tensioner, oil service.
repairOptions.longevity: 3–6 years. Pragmatic mid‑life refresh addressing all three linked failure points.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement – Rebuilt / Reconditioned Ingenium Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuild with upgraded timing components, revised EGR system, thoroughly cleaned or replaced DPF, new oil pump. Supply & fit.
repairOptions.longevity: 5–10+ years. The definitive fix that restores factory reliability.

problems.recommendation: When multiple emission‑system problems have already caused secondary wear to the engine internals — particularly timing chain stretch from prolonged oil dilution — a rebuilt Ingenium unit with updated components is often more cost‑effective than sequentially repairing the EGR, DPF and chain. This is especially true on higher‑mileage cars where each individual repair can cost more than the vehicle's remaining value.
problems.cta: → Compare prices on reconditioned Ingenium engines with upgraded EGR & DPF components

problems.h4: Defender Engine Problems – From 200Tdi to Puma, Every Generation Has Its Weak Points
problems.rootCause: Each Defender engine generation has distinct failure patterns. 200Tdi/300Tdi suffer head gasket failure, timing belt degradation, and oil leaks from ageing seals. The Td5 develops injector harness oil contamination (oil wicking through the wiring loom into the ECU), fuel pump failures, and cracked exhaust manifolds. The Puma 2.4/2.2 TDCi suffers injector seal failure (causing oil dilution), EGR valve clogging, and VNT turbo mechanism sticking.

repairOptions.tier: Minor – Puma injector seals & EGR clean
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New injector seals, EGR valve cleaning, oil and filter change.
repairOptions.longevity: 2–4 years. Resolves the oil dilution and smoke issues on Puma engines.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate – Td5 injector harness & head gasket
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New injector wiring loom, head gasket replacement, cylinder head inspection, fuel pump check.
repairOptions.longevity: 3–7 years. Addresses the Td5's notorious oil‑in‑loom problem.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuild with upgraded head gasket, new timing belt/chain, new injector seals, cleaned EGR system (Puma), calibrated turbo assembly. Bench‑tested with 12‑month warranty. Supply & fit.
repairOptions.longevity: 5–10+ years. Resets engine life for another decade of work.

problems.recommendation: For many Defender owners — especially those using their vehicle for overlanding, farming, or commercial work — a professionally rebuilt engine with upgraded components provides better long‑term value than repeated repairs on an ageing original unit. The Puma engines in particular benefit from a specialist rebuild that addresses the injector seal and EGR weaknesses in one go. For classic Tdi owners, a rebuilt unit preserves originality while eliminating the accumulated wear of 25+ years.
problems.cta: → Find rebuilt Land Rover Defender engines from UK specialists

problems.h4: Named after the specific failure (e.g., "Timing Chain Failure – Why {{BRAND}} Engines Fail Prematurely")
problems.cta: A short action line like "Compare rebuilt {{BRAND}} engine prices with upgraded [component]

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Land Rover
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing a timing chain rattle on your Discovery Sport, a seized crankshaft on your Range Rover Sport, or a tired Td5 in your Defender, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Land Rover engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, upgraded components addressing known failure points, and nationwide delivery. Compare prices today and get your Land Rover back on the road — without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Land Rover Engine Replacement Prices Now
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates (Land Rover main dealers typically charge £200–£240 per hour) and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network (independent Land Rover specialists typically charge £60–£100 per hour). Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Land Rover model and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty — always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Land Rover Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every Land Rover engine type available through our UK network, with typical price ranges and recommendations based on your vehicle’s age, condition and your budget.

types.title: Land Rover Reconditioned Engines (Recon)A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM-spec parts. Best for owners who want factory reliability without the factory price.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,500 – £4,500 (depending on engine code)
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Land Rover engine prices

types.title: Land Rover Rebuilt EnginesA complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points – such as reinforced timing chains, uprated bearings and revised oil pumps – for a longer service life than a standard replacement.
types.cta: Get rebuilt Land Rover engine quotes

types.title: Used Land Rover EnginesA tested, low-mileage take-out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available.
types.cta: Check used Land Rover engine availability

types.title: Land Rover Remanufactured EnginesA factory-spec, as-new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for performance models like Range Rover Supercharged or SDV6.
types.cta: Compare remanufactured Land Rover engine costs

types.title: Land Rover Refurbished EnginesA lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn’t had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older Defender or Discovery models where a full recon isn’t economical.
types.cta: View refurbished Land Rover engine options

types.title: Land Rover Supply & Fit EnginesYour replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Land Rover specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £500 – £1,500 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Land Rover engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12-month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Land Rover model today.

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Land Rover Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Land Rover engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Land Rover specialists.

entries.title: 204DTD / AJ200D 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: The most common modern Land Rover engine code. Fitted to Discovery Sport (L550), Range Rover Evoque (L538), Discovery 5, Velar, and Range Rover Sport. Jaguar vehicles use the same engine under code AJ200D. Prone to timing-chain stretch, oil dilution from incomplete DPF regeneration, and turbocharger failure - often before 80,000 miles. UK-rebuilt units include upgraded timing chain kits and revised oil pump assemblies.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 204DTD / AJ200D Land Rover engine replacement

entries.title: 204DTA 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: The higher-output variant of the Ingenium 2.0 diesel, producing 180–240 HP. Used in Range Rover Evoque, Discovery Sport, and Velar. Shares the timing-chain and oil-dilution issues of the 204DTD but also suffers high-pressure fuel pump failures under sustained load. Our rebuilt units include updated HPFP components and reinforced chain tensioners.

entries.title: AJ20D6 3.0L Diesel
entries.description: The 3.0-litre Ingenium six-cylinder diesel, producing 250–300 HP. Found in Range Rover (L405, 2018+), Range Rover Sport (L494, 2018+), and Discovery 5. Early examples suffered crankshaft bearing wear - later revisions improved this, but high-mileage units still present spun-bearing failures. Rebuilds include uprated bearing shells and modified oil supply lines.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ20D6 3.0L Land Rover diesel engines

entries.title: 276DT 2.7L TDV6 Diesel
entries.description: The original TDV6. Fitted to Discovery 3 (L319, 2004–2009) and early Range Rover Sport (L320, 2005–2009). Notorious for catastrophic crankshaft failure, oil-pump housing disintegration, and snapped timing belts - often a complete engine write-off when it occurs. UK-rebuilt 276DT engines include uprated crankshaft bearings and revised oil-pump assemblies.
entries.cta: Compare 276DT TDV6 Land Rover engine replacement costs

entries.title: 306DT 3.0L TDV6 Diesel
entries.description: The 3.0-litre TDV6 producing 211–245 HP. Used in Discovery 4 (L319), Range Rover Sport (L320, 2009–2013), and Range Rover (L322). Shares the crankshaft-bearing vulnerability of the 2.7, with additional EGR cooler and turbo actuator failures. Rebuilt engines include new bearing shells, upgraded EGR components, and tested turbo assemblies.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 306DT 3.0L Land Rover TDV6 engines

entries.title: 306DTX 3.0L SDV6 Diesel
entries.description: The sequential twin-turbo SDV6 producing 250–306 HP - the most powerful diesel of the TDV6 era. Fitted to Discovery 4, Range Rover Sport (2012–2018), and Range Rover (L405, 2013–2018). Crankshaft failure remains the critical risk; secondary turbocharger actuator seizure is also common on higher-mileage examples. Our rebuilt units address both with upgraded rotating assemblies and revised turbo mechanisms.
entries.cta: Compare 306DTX SDV6 engine prices from UK specialists

entries.title: ZSD-424 2.4L Puma TDCi Diesel
entries.description: The Ford Duratorq-based Puma engine producing 122 HP. Fitted to Land Rover Defender 90/110/130 (2007–2011). Well-documented for injector seal failure (causing oil dilution), EGR valve clogging, and VNT turbo mechanism sticking. UK-rebuilt Puma engines include new injector seals, cleaned/calibrated turbo assemblies, and revised EGR components with improved cooling.
entries.cta: Get quotes for ZSD-424 Land Rover Defender Puma engine replacement

entries.title: 244DT 2.4L Diesel
entries.description: A badge-engineered variant of the ZSD-424 used across Defender Station Wagon, Pickup, Cabrio, and Platform/Chassis models. Shares the injector-seal and VNT turbo issues of the ZSD-424. Rebuilt units include upgraded injector seals and tested turbocharger assemblies, with most UK suppliers offering a 12-month warranty on all components.
entries.cta: Compare 244DT Land Rover Defender engine prices

entries.title: 508PN 5.0L Supercharged V8 Petrol
entries.description: Jaguar Land Rover's flagship AJ133 5.0-litre supercharged V8, producing 375–510 HP. Fitted to Range Rover (L322/L405), Range Rover Sport (L320/L494), and SVAutobiography models from 2009 onwards. The primary failure point is timing-chain tensioner wear - producing a distinctive cold-start rattle that worsens progressively. Supercharger coupler wear, water-pump leakage, and coolant-pipe degradation are also common at higher mileages. UK-rebuilt 508PN engines include upgraded timing-chain kits with reinforced tensioners, revised water pumps, and new supercharger couplers as standard.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 508PN 5.0 Supercharged Land Rover V8 engines

entries.title: 448PN 4.4L V8 Petrol
entries.description: The naturally aspirated 4.4-litre V8 producing 299 HP. Used in Discovery 3 (L319, 2004–2009) and Range Rover Sport (L320, 2005–2009). Timing-chain guide-rail disintegration and VANOS solenoid failure are the most common reasons for replacement. Our rebuilt units include reinforced guide rails, updated timing components, and new VANOS solenoids calibrated to factory specifications.
entries.cta: Compare 448PN Land Rover V8 engine replacement prices

entries.title: 224DT 2.2L Diesel
entries.description: Used in Freelander 2 (L359, 2006–2014) and early Range Rover Evoque (L538, 2011–2015). Based on the Ford Duratorq DW12 engine. Generally reliable compared to other Land Rover engines, but EGR valve failure and turbo oil-feed pipe blockages appear at higher mileage. Rebuilt units include upgraded EGR components and revised turbo oil supply lines.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 224DT 2.2L Land Rover diesel engines

entries.title: DW12BTED4 2.7L Diesel
entries.description: A PSA/Ford co-developed 2.7-litre V6 diesel used in early Discovery 3 and Range Rover Sport models. Similar in character to the 276DT TDV6 but predates the crankshaft-failure reputation. Common issues include EGR valve clogging and turbocharger oil leaks at high mileage. Rebuilt engines include tested turbo assemblies and updated EGR components.
entries.cta: Compare DW12BTED4 Land Rover engine prices from UK suppliers

entries.title: 306PS 3.0L Supercharged V6 Petrol
entries.description: The 3.0-litre supercharged V6 petrol producing 340–380 HP. Fitted to Range Rover Sport (L494, 2014+) and Range Rover (L405). Less common than the diesel variants but timing-chain tensioner wear and supercharger-related issues affect higher-mileage examples. faq.h2: Land Rover Engine Replacement – Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Quick, honest answers to the most common questions Land Rover owners ask before comparing engine replacement prices and finding a UK specialist.

items.question: How much does a Land Rover engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: The cost of a Land Rover engine replacement depends on your engine code, condition, and fitting requirements. Rebuilt units typically range from £1,500 for a Defender 300Tdi to £8,500 for a Range Rover 5.0 Supercharged V8. Reconditioned engines sit around £1,500 – £5,000. Fitting generally adds £500 – £1,500 depending on your location. We always recommend comparing three quotes before committing.
items.cta: Compare Land Rover engine replacement prices now for an accurate, no‑obligation quote tailored to your vehicle.

items.question: What affects the cost of a Land Rover engine replacement?
items.answer: Four things influence the price: engine size (a 3.0 SDV6 costs more than a 2.2 diesel), condition (used is cheaper, rebuilt is pricier but lasts longer), brand and age of your vehicle (newer, complex engines like the Ingenium require specialist labour), and whether you choose supply‑only or supply‑and‑fit. Location also matters - fitting costs in London can be 20‑30% higher than elsewhere. Our tool breaks down these costs for you.
items.cta: Check Land Rover engine supply and fit quotes with full price transparency.

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Land Rover?
items.answer: In most cases yes, particularly with modern Land Rovers where chassis, gearbox and bodywork remain solid while the engine fails prematurely. A rebuilt engine with upgraded timing‑chain components or uprated bearings often gives a longer service life than the original unit. The alternative - buying a replacement vehicle - usually costs far more. If your Land Rover is otherwise in good condition and holds value (Defender, Range Rover), engine replacement is almost always worth it.
items.cta: Use our free comparison tool to see whether a rebuilt or used Land Rover engine makes financial sense.

items.question: What’s included in a Land Rover engine supply and fit service?
items.answer: Supply and fit covers sourcing the correct rebuilt, reconditioned or used engine for your Land Rover, removing your old unit, installing the replacement, new fluids, filters, and a workmanship warranty on top of the engine warranty. Most UK specialists also dispose of your old engine and test‑drive the vehicle afterwards. Typical fitting costs range from £500 to £1,500 depending on the model and garage location.
items.cta: Compare Land Rover engine supply and fit deals from trusted UK specialists in minutes.

items.question: How long does a Land Rover engine replacement take?
items.answer: For a like‑for‑like swap on a common model like a Discovery Sport or Freelander 2, most UK garages complete the job in 3–5 working days once the replacement engine is on site. More complex models (Range Rover L405, newer Defender) can take 5–7 days. The biggest variable is engine availability - our network includes suppliers who stock the most regularly replaced Land Rover engine codes, cutting waiting times significantly.
items.cta: Get quotes now and ask each supplier about their typical lead time.

items.question: Do reconditioned Land Rover engines come with warranty?
items.answer: Yes. Every Land Rover replacement engine sourced through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Many suppliers offer extended 24‑month coverage on rebuilt and remanufactured units. The warranty covers all major internal components including the crankshaft, pistons, timing chain, and oil pump - reflecting the confidence our specialists have in their work. Always confirm exact warranty terms directly with your chosen supplier before purchase.
items.cta: Find reconditioned Land Rover engines with comprehensive warranty cover.

items.question: Should I choose a used or reconditioned Land Rover engine?
items.answer: It depends on your budget and how long you plan to keep your Land Rover. A used engine is cheaper upfront (£800 – £3,000) and works well if you need a quick, temporary fix or are selling soon. A reconditioned engine costs more (£1,500 – £5,000) but includes new components, a warranty, and should last another 100,000+ miles. If your Land Rover has one of the well‑known failure‑prone engines (TDV6, early Ingenium), a reconditioned unit with upgraded parts is strongly recommended.
items.cta: Compare both options with live prices from UK Land Rover engine specialists.

items.question: Where can I find Land Rover engine specialists near me?
items.answer: EngineMarket connects you with a UK‑wide network of independent Land Rover engine specialists, from Defender experts in the Midlands to Range Rover rebuilders in Scotland and the South East. Simply enter your registration number and we'll match you with nearby suppliers who stock your specific engine code and offer fitting services. Many also provide nationwide delivery if you already have a trusted garage.
items.cta: Compare Land Rover engine dealers and specialists near you.

items.question: What are the most common Land Rover engine failures?
items.answer: The top failures that drive owners to seek replacement include timing‑chain stretch and tensioner failure (Ingenium 2.0 diesel and 5.0 V8), crankshaft bearing seizure (TDV6 and SDV6 2.7/3.0), oil dilution from DPF regeneration issues (Ingenium), turbocharger failure (across multiple diesel families), and EGR/DPF blockage on older models. Early detection rarely prevents these failures from turning catastrophic, which is why replacement - rather than repair - is the most common outcome.
items.cta: Get quotes for a rebuilt Land Rover engine designed to address these failures permanently.

items.question: How can I get the best price on a Land Rover engine?
items.answer: Compare multiple quotes. Prices vary significantly between UK Land Rover engine suppliers - we've seen differences of £1,000+ on the same engine code. Consider a used engine if your budget is tight or a reconditioned unit with upgraded components if you plan to keep your Land Rover long‑term. Ask about supply‑only if you already have a trusted garage, and always check whether fitting, delivery and VAT are included in the quoted price.
items.cta: Use our free tool to compare Land Rover engine prices from multiple suppliers instantly and secure the best deal.
